findings.name: overfitting
findings.headline: Volatility grew 12.3× by the final checkpoint — possible overfitting
findings.observation: Mean Volatility climbed from 0.01943 (median of epochs ['ep1', 'ep5', 'ep15']) to 0.2383 at 'ep30'.
findings.why_it_matters: A late-training jump in Volatility means feature effects are now strongly sample-dependent — the model is memorising individual examples rather than generalising.
findings.recommendation: Consider early stopping at an earlier checkpoint, adding regularisation, or evaluating held-out generalisation.

findings.name: trust_instability
findings.headline: 17/30 features are unstable across checkpoints
findings.observation: More than half (57%) of features changed archetype between checkpoints (similarity-weighted stability < 0.5).
findings.why_it_matters: High INVESTIGATE rate suggests the model has not settled into stable feature roles — either it is still training, or the data is noisy enough that different epochs use the features differently.
findings.recommendation: Train for more epochs, or use a learning-rate schedule that converges sooner. If accuracy is good but features are unstable, the model is an ensemble in disguise.

findings.name: capacity
findings.headline: Healthy archetype distribution
findings.why_it_matters: No archetype dominates — the model has a diverse mix of feature roles.

findings.name: co_sensitivity
findings.headline: Co-Sensitivity refused to recommend any prune (2 groups found, but no prune-safe group)
findings.observation: k=2 functional groups, silhouette=0.81, permutation p=0.000, bootstrap ARI=1.00, best group NC fraction=14%.  Abort triggered because: no group has >50% Noise Candidates (best=14%).
findings.why_it_matters: Co-Sensitivity clusters features by gradient similarity. It only recommends pruning when a whole group is noise-dominated (>50%) AND the clustering itself is statistically supported. Refusing to prune is the SAFE outcome — the alternative is removing useful features.
findings.recommendation: Pruning is not warranted from this run. If you need to compress the model, use magnitude-based or movement pruning instead.

findings.name: trust_keep_recommended
findings.headline: 6 features are stably important across all checkpoints
findings.observation: These features retained the same useful archetype (Workhorse / Catalyst / Stable Contributor) at every checkpoint.
findings.why_it_matters: High-stability + high-importance features are the backbone of the model. Removing or changing them will be felt in accuracy.
findings.recommendation: Treat these as load-bearing. Protect with extra logging in production; do not prune.
